slides.title: Brand Identity at a Glance
body: Developer-first
Clean
Approachable
Technical
Modern
Visual style: light-mode technical with deep navy dark sections
First impression: this is auth for builders who don't want to build auth

slides.title: Core Positioning
body: Positioning: No-code external identity platform for apps, AI agents, and MCP servers — drag and drop
Category play: External IAM / CIAM — positioning ahead of Auth0/Okta as the developer-native modern alternative
Wedge message: Drag & drop identity federation — makes the hard thing (auth) feel trivially easy
Primary value prop: One platform for all external identities — users, B2B customers, partners, AI agents
Secondary value prop: No-code flows + full SDK coverage = works for both no-code builders and pro devs

slides.title: ICP & Buyer Profile
slides.subtitle: Who they are selling to and how they speak to them
body: Primary buyer: Engineering leads, CTOs, and developer-founders at B2B SaaS and consumer apps
Industries targeted: SaaS, fintech, consumer apps, enterprise software — anyone building user-facing auth
Company stage/size: Startup to mid-market — Sign up CTA (no demo required) confirms self-serve PLG entry
Buyer sophistication: High technical — SDK links, Quick start docs, API reference all above the fold in nav
Pain trigger: Engineering team spending weeks building auth flows instead of shipping product features
Objection pre-empted: GoFundMe case study answers — will this work at scale in production for real companies

slides.title: Messaging Architecture
body: H1: Drag & drop Identity federation (rotating typewriter — shows multiple use cases)
Key H2: One platform for all your external identities
Key H2: Our no-code CIAM platform
Key H2: Power endless external identity use cases
Sub-headline: Auth that meets devs where they are

slides.title: Messaging Framework Deep Dive
slides.subtitle: How they structure the narrative top to bottom
body: Hook (above fold): Typewriter H1 cycles through use cases — identity federation, B2B auth, AI agents — shows range immediately
Problem frame: Auth is complex, time-consuming, and pulls engineers off product work — implicit not explicit
Solution introduction: Drag & drop no-code flows + SDK coverage = build in minutes not weeks
Proof point: Powering auth for 1000+ organizations in production — scale + trust signal before any feature pitch
Feature-to-benefit: No-code flows → ship faster; AI agent support → future-proof your auth stack now
Closing argument: Ready for liftoff? — energetic, launch metaphor, Sign up CTA with zero friction

slides.title: GTM Motion & CTA Strategy
body: GTM motion: Hybrid PLG + SLG — Sign up (free, no card) for developers, Book a demo for enterprise buyers
Primary CTA: Sign up → /sign-up — PLG self-serve confirms developer-led adoption motion
Secondary CTA: Book a demo → /demo — sales layer for enterprise or complex multi-tenant deals
Lead magnet: Quick start docs, SDK explorer, G2 reviews — developers evaluate by building, not by reading
Trust signals: 1000+ organizations in production, GoFundMe case study, G2 badge — all before any conversion ask
Friction level: Very low — Sign up is the primary CTA, no form visible, no credit card language

slides.title: Visual Identity
body: Illustration style: Technical product UI with wire/flow diagrams — shows the actual drag-and-drop builder metaphor
Why this style: Makes an abstract concept (identity flows) instantly tangible — the product IS the illustration
Animation intent: Load-time staggered entrance with spring physics — energetic, playful, dev-friendly feel
Hero hierarchy: Text-first with animated typewriter — H1 cycles use cases before visuals render
Product shown directly: Yes — hero likely shows the drag-and-drop flow builder UI, high fidelity product preview

slides.title: Animation Stack
slides.subtitle: How it is built technically
body: Primary stack: CSS Keyframes (17 found) + GSAP + Lottie + Rive — heavy multi-library approach
Scroll animations: Load-time staggered hero entrance — most animations fire on page load, not scroll
CSS keyframes: Navbar scale/fade/enter/exit animations, typewriter cursor blink, nav menu slide transitions
Spring physics: cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) — overshoot spring used for element entrances, feels bouncy and alive
Custom easing: cubic-bezier(.59,0,.06,1) — sharp deceleration on transitions, snappy and technical feel

slides.title: 3 Animation Techniques to Steal
body: 1. Typewriter H1 with cursor blink: Cycles through multiple use cases in one headline — shows product range without extra copy; implement with CSS animation type-animation_blink + JS text cycling on a timed interval
2. Spring entrance cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1): Elements overshoot then settle — feels alive and developer-playful; apply to any hero element entrance with transform: translateY + this easing
3. Wire connector SVG illustrations: CSS gradient wires connecting UI nodes — shows product flow visually; implement with SVG path + linearGradient fill using brand cyan to purple gradient

slides.title: Illustration System Deep Dive
body: Style: Technical product UI diagrams — flow builders, wire connectors, node-based identity flows
Custom or library: Custom — wire gradient CSS vars (--leftWireId, --rightWireId) confirm bespoke illustration system
Built for animation: Yes — Rive and Lottie detected, wire connector SVGs with gradient fills designed for motion
Color in illustrations: Cyan (#53BDE4) as the primary connector/highlight, dark navy as base, warm orange as accent node
Depth technique: Multi-layer SVG z-stacking with gradient overlays — wire gradients fade in/out to create depth

slides.title: What to Steal / What to Avoid
body: ✓ Steal: Typewriter H1 cycling through use cases — shows product range in one headline without clutter
✓ Steal: Sign up as primary CTA — low-friction PLG entry removes all barriers for developer evaluation
✓ Steal: SDK grid with 14 languages — comprehensive coverage displayed upfront builds developer confidence immediately
✗ Avoid: Nav is too sparse — only 3 links (Customers, Sign up, Book a demo) hides product depth from mid-funnel visitors
✗ Avoid: Drag & drop H1 loses context without animation — in static/scraped form it reads as incomplete sentence

slides.title: How to Replicate This Design System
slides.subtitle: Minimum viable build to get 80% of the visual impact
body: Style rule: Technical flow diagrams with wire connectors — show the product as the illustration, no metaphor needed
Color system: 1 deep navy base + 1 bright cyan primary + 1 warm accent (orange) + off-white for light sections
Animation rule: Spring entrance (overshoot cubic-bezier) for hero, CSS keyframes for nav, Lottie for product demos
Tools: Figma for flow diagrams, CSS gradients for wire effects, Rive for interactive product previews
Build first: The drag-and-drop flow builder hero illustration — it IS the product pitch and delivers 70% of the impact

slides.title: Competitive Signals
body: Category owned: External IAM / CIAM — positioning as modern no-code alternative to Auth0, Okta, and Clerk
Benchmark 1: Auth0/Okta — Descope differentiates on no-code flows and price; Auth0 requires heavy developer customization
Benchmark 2: Clerk — similar dev-first positioning but Descope adds B2B multi-tenant + AI agent support Clerk lacks
JTBD framing: Let my engineering team ship product features instead of building and maintaining authentication
Differentiation signal: First CIAM platform to natively support AI agents and MCP servers — no competitor claims this yet

slides.title: Design–Copy Harmony
body: Illustration reinforces headline: Yes — drag-and-drop builder visual directly shows what Drag & drop identity means
Metaphor system: Building blocks and connections — flows, wires, nodes all reinforce the platform-as-assembler concept
Color as narrative: Light sections (neutral, open) → dark navy sections (serious, enterprise) → cyan highlights (action)
Icon/illustration consistency: Wire SVGs match flow diagrams match SDK icons — single technical illustration register
Design principle this site nails: Product UI as hero illustration — removes the gap between promise and proof instantly

slides.quote: Descope is the no-code external identity platform for developers who want to ship auth in hours not weeks — the only CIAM built for users, B2B customers, partners, and AI agents from a single drag-and-drop interface.
